posts.content:
The more experience, the better the compliance. Right?

Wrong.

In India's First Independent MFD Risk Profiling Study, we found something that challenges a deeply held assumption:

Average compliance score by experience:
→ Less than 2 years experience: Score 32.0
→ 2-5 years experience: Score 31.6
→ 5-10 years experience: Score 27.7
→ More than 15 years experience: Score 29.1
→ 10-15 years experience: Score 21.3 (LOWEST of all groups)

The most experienced cohort scores the lowest. Why?

Because long-standing habits are the hardest to change. Because "this is how I've always done it" becomes the default answer. Because informal processes that worked for years do not meet SEBI's March 2026 documentation requirements.

Newer MFDs are building compliance habits from scratch — the right way.
Experienced MFDs are carrying the weight of habits built before the new requirements existed.

Compliance is not about how long you've been doing this. It is about whether your process matches what SEBI requires today.

Where does your process stand? Participate in the Study use the link in first comment below 👇 

#MFD #MutualFunds #SEBI #RiskProfiling #Compliance #PulseLabsResearch #MFDIndia #FinancialPlanning

Faizal Baig, Sadique Neelgund, QPFP®, Network FP, FIFA India, Mutual Fund Round Table (MFRT), Association of Mutual Funds in India (AMFI), HDFC Mutual Fund, Nippon India Mutual Fund, SBI MUTUAL FUND, ICICI Prudential AMC Ltd, Shubham Gupta, krishan sharma, Pragati Kapoor - ETWealth Editor, Mint, Cafemutual, Vijay Venkatram, National Institute of Securities Markets (NISM)
posts.summary: The more experience, the better the compliance. Right? Wrong.

posts.content:
If AI is transforming Wealth Management, why does most of the Industry still feel stuck?

That’s the question Sohini Bagchi  at TechCircle put to me last week.

The hard problem in WealthTech isn’t AI. It’s the reporting lag, proprietary data formats, the gap between markets that move in seconds and portfolio reports that move in days. 

Wealth Managers end up as forensic scientists, stitching together fragmented data instead of sitting with their clients. The data is everywhere and useful nowhere.

In the piece I cover three things every WealthTech builder should be thinking about:

→ Data as a Product — moving from messy silos to live, decision-ready data that’s ready the moment it’s needed

→ Architecture over Add-ons — making AI a first-class citizen and embedding governance at the core, not bolted on before the next audit

→ The Unglamorous Win — where AI is actually delivering the most immediate impact right now, and it’s not where most people expect.

I believe that the next wave of winners in Indian WealthTech won’t be the ones with the flashiest models. They’ll be the ones who did the unglamorous work underneath.

At Centricity, we’re working aggressively to build the next gen WealthTech experience with Data & AI integrated as the core layer — a state-of-the-art, multi-product, multi-asset Wealth Management platform where the above points isn’t the vision. It’s the foundation.

Full piece linked in the comments, worth a read if you’re building, advising, or investing in this space.

Manu Awasthy Pushpendra Singh  Aditya Shankar Manish Sharma Gaurav Tiwari  Gaurav Singh Rashmi S. Kashyap Nirmal Rewaria Ishkaran Chhabra  Peeyush Srivastava  Nikhil Golatkar Shvetank Mishra Sanaa Zia Khan  Anjali B.  
  
TechCircle  Centricity WealthTech  

#WealthTech #AI #FinTech #Data #Centricity #AIStrategy #InvestmentStrategy | 15 comments on LinkedIn

posts.content:
𝐈𝐬 𝐚 𝐅𝐚𝐦𝐢𝐥𝐲 𝐎𝐟𝐟𝐢𝐜𝐞 𝐎𝐧𝐥𝐲 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐔𝐥𝐭𝐫𝐚 𝐖𝐞𝐚𝐥𝐭𝐡𝐲?

Most people associate a family office with billion dollar balance sheets and private investment teams.

That view misses the point.

A family office is not defined by scale. It is defined by structure.

At its core, a family office is a coordinated way to manage wealth, risk, tax planning, succession, and governance. Whether a family has one operating business and a few investments, or a diversified global portfolio, the need for alignment remains the same.

Here is what a family office mindset creates.

- Central visibility across assets instead of fragmented information
- Coordinated tax and estate planning rather than reactive compliance
- Structured investment policy instead of opportunity driven decisions
- Clear governance between family members and business management
- Long term continuity across generations

It does not always mean a large team or a standalone entity. It can begin as a small, disciplined structure with defined reporting, documented policies, and aligned advisors.

Every family with meaningful assets already operates a family office in some form. The question is whether it is intentional or accidental.


#India #Familyoffice #Law #Future  | 28 comments on LinkedIn

posts.content:
I spent 3 hours yesterday watching our team manually copy data from PDFs into spreadsheets

That's when it hit me — we're still doing tasks in 2026 that AI agents solved in 2024

While everyone's debating whether AI will "replace jobs," smart teams are already using AI agents to eliminate the soul-crushing work nobody wanted to do anyway

Here are 20 real-world use cases you can build with OpenClaw today:

For Your Business Operations: → Automated Research Assistant that searches web, gathers sources, and compiles structured reports automatically → Email Triage & Smart Reply Drafter that reads your inbox, categorizes emails, and drafts contextual responses for approval → Data Entry & Form Processing Bot that extracts data from PDFs/CSVs and populates spreadsheets or databases

For Your Team's Productivity: → Meeting Preparation Assistant that collects company info, previous notes, agenda points, and talking insights → Personal Knowledge Base Agent (RAG) that indexes your documents and answers questions from your private data → Cross-App Workflow Automation Agent connecting Slack, Notion, Sheets, and APIs to execute multi-step workflows

For Content & Marketing: → Content Repurposing Automation that converts one blog into posts, tweets, reels scripts, and email newsletters → Social Media Monitoring & Response Drafting that tracks mentions, trends, and drafts engagement replies automatically → Lead Generation & Qualification that scrapes business data, filters prospects, and prepares outreach-ready summaries

For Development & Tech Teams: → Autonomous DevOps Monitoring Assistant that observes system logs, detects issues and suggests safe remediation actions → Bug Investigation & Log Analyzer that reads logs, detects anomalies, and proposes reproduction steps → Codebase Exploration & Explanation that navigates repositories, explains architecture, & suggests improvements

For Strategic Work: → Competitive Intelligence Tracker monitoring competitor websites, updates, changes, and generating insight reports → Automated Business Report Generator pulling analytics data and creating human-readable summary dashboards → Travel & Itinerary Planning Assistant researching flights, hotels, budgets, and generating full travel plans

For Specialized Workflows: → Resume Customization & Job Matching that matches job descriptions with resumes and generates tailored applications → Customer Support Ticket Classifier analyzing support queries, detecting intent, and routing to correct teams → Document Compliance & Policy Checker scanning contracts/docs to ensure required clauses and compliance standards → Study Planner & Task Scheduler building adaptive daily study plans based on syllabus and deadlines → Knowledge Base Auto-Updater

The best part?
These aren't futuristic concepts
Teams are building and deploying these TODAY

Are you still manually doing work that an agent could handle, or are you building the systems that free your team to do work that actually matters? | 79 comments on LinkedIn

posts.content:
🚀 From Idea to Impact: Where Vision Meets Execution

India’s startup ecosystem is not just growing… it is transforming the future of the nation.
With 100,000+ DPIIT-recognized startups, rising unicorns, and strong government initiatives, Bharat is steadily becoming a global innovation powerhouse.

Amidst this momentum, ecosystems like the @Ideation Innovation & Incubation (I-3) Foundation, Indian Institute of Technology (Banaras Hindu University), Varanasi  are playing a crucial role in shaping the next generation of founders.

Programs like:
• RKVY-RAFTAAR Agribusiness Incubator, IIT (BHU) –RAFTAAR Agribusiness Incubator – strengthening agri startups
• Joint Incubation Center – connecting ideas with industry
• Cisco  thingQbator – accelerating AI & tech innovation
• E-Cell IIT BHU  – nurturing entrepreneurial mindset

These are not just initiatives—
👉 They are gateways to real-world impact.

But here’s what every founder must understand:

💡 India is giving opportunities like never before…
👉 But only those who execute will benefit.

Recent trends clearly show:
	•	🚀 Rapid growth in AI & Agritech startups
	•	🌱 Increased focus on sustainable and rural innovations
	•	💼 Strong push for MSME and startup funding ecosystems

Yet, the reality remains unchanged:

🔥 Funding doesn’t build startups… founders do
🔥 Ideas don’t scale… execution does
🔥 Ecosystems support… but hustle wins

If you are an entrepreneur today—this is your time.

👉 Start before you feel ready
👉 Leverage platforms like IIT BHU
👉 Learn, adapt, and execute relentlessly

Because in this new India
Opportunities are everywhere… but success belongs to those who act.

Let’s not just dream
Let’s build the future.

#StartupIndia #Innovation #Entrepreneurship #IITBHU #AgriTech #AI #MSME #StartupEcosystem #Founders #BuildInPublic #NewIndia #DhanBooks

EXIM MATTERS – EDITORIAL 41
Trade with Insight

This week, Exim Matters reaches you a day later than usual. We appreciate your patience — and perhaps this delay itself reflects the central theme of… | Padmanabhan R R
posts.content:
EXIM MATTERS – EDITORIAL 41
Trade with Insight

This week, Exim Matters reaches you a day later than usual. We appreciate your patience — and perhaps this delay itself reflects the central theme of this edition: in trade, delays rarely arise from intent, but from execution gaps.
Our lead story examines a critical but often overlooked issue — EPCG delays after licence approval. On paper, approvals are faster, digital, and efficient. In practice, however, the process does not end with licence issuance. Where Bank Guarantees are required, execution becomes a multi-layered coordination exercise between importer, bank, and Customs. Format mismatches, internal bank workflows, and confirmation requirements can easily add several days of delay. The insight is clear: in today’s trade environment, efficiency is no longer defined by approvals alone, but by the precision of last-mile execution.
This theme of practical friction versus theoretical clarity runs through the rest of the edition.
The discussion on the Kelly’s impasse prompts us to question whether certain legacy positions continue to serve present-day trade realities. Our piece on GST risks for landlords highlights how compliance failures, even indirect ones, can have unintended consequences. The principle of lex non cogit ad impossibilia reminds us that law must remain grounded in practical feasibility.
On a broader canvas, we celebrate India’s everyday excellence, where quiet consistency continues to build global credibility. And in a surprising but telling example, the story of the USA importing cow dung from India illustrates how even the most unexpected products can find global relevance when positioned correctly.
Together, these perspectives reinforce a simple but important truth:
In trade, success lies not just in policy, but in execution — not just in approval, but in alignment. The magazine can be viewed at https://lnkd.in/g2rQgqfa
Editorial Team
Exim Matters
Connecting Policy, Practice & People in Global Trade
Guided by experience. Shared for the trade community.

posts.author_name:
There is one sector which is quietly picking up pace in this volatile market..



It's the Power Sector.

👉 Electricity is becoming the most critical infrastructure of the AI economy.

⚡ WHAT’S… | Piyali Parashari | 52 comments
posts.content:
There is one sector which is quietly picking up pace in this volatile market..



It's the Power Sector.

👉 Electricity is becoming the most critical infrastructure of the AI economy.

⚡ WHAT’S CHANGING?
India’s power sector is entering a multi-decade capex and demand supercycle driven by three factors 

1. HIGH DEMAND FROM AI & DATA CENTERS
India data center capacity has already grown 3x (2020–2025)

The market is expected to grow from $9 billion → $ 31 billion in a few years.
Data centers may consume 6% of India’s electricity by 2030

👉 These are 24/7, high-density power loads something which grids have not handled before.

2. MASSIVE INVESTMENT UNDERWAY
🇮🇳 In the Union Budget 2026 ₹12.2 lakh crore infra led capex was allocated.

₹2.99 lakh crore allocation to power sector ( increased by 39% YoY)

Renewable ministry allocation was up 30% YoY

₹23–24 lakh crore for infra → ₹6–7 lakh crore flowing into renewables alone

₹9 lakh crore planned in transmission & distribution (T&D) ⚡

3. RENEWABLE EXPANSION
India added 50 GW renewables in FY26 and generation jumped 24% in 2025
Target: 500 GW capacity by 2030

🟢Corporate demand (AI companies now want green power)
With policy support and financing it's scaling rapidly. 


⚫ But Fossil Fuels Are NOT Going Away
Despite the green push:
India still relies heavily on thermal power. Since renewables are intermittent and Data centers need 100% uptime, non renewable energy still remains in demand 

Nuclear power is emerging as long-term clean baseload (100 GW target by 2047)

⚠️ The REAL BOTTLENECK today is the  GRID, 
This is where the biggest opportunity (and risk) lies:

1. Transmission lag
Renewable capacity is growing faster than grid readiness

2. Distribution stress
DISCOM financial health is still weak and Smart metering & reforms are  underway

3. Data center clustering risk
Massive localized load → leads to grid instability

👉Adani is committing $100B to AI + data centers + renewables.

👉Reliance investing $110B in AI infrastructure

👉States are planning GW-scale data center parks
Located near:
Power hubs
Coastal regions
Industrial corridors

📊 WHAT THIS MEANS FOR INVESTORS 

⚡ The future is about who can generate, store, and deliver reliable electricity at scale.

And countries that solve this — will lead the AI era.

The power sector is entering a multi-decade upcycle and definitely a high growth sector for the future

PS Only for educational purposes. 

Image Credit. Respective Owner
